name: Paracetamol
use_case: Relief of mild to moderate pain and reduction of fever. Used for headaches, toothaches, muscle aches, colds, and flu.
mechanism: Inhibits prostaglandin synthesis centrally; exact mechanism incompletely understood.

common_dosages.adult: 500mg–1000mg every 4–6 hours. Maximum 4g/day.
common_dosages.elderly: 500mg every 6 hours; avoid prolonged high doses.

age_warnings.pediatric: Weight-based dosing mandatory in children. Do not exceed 5 doses in 24 hours.
age_warnings.geriatric: Use minimum effective dose in elderly.
age_warnings.pregnancy: Category B – considered safe at therapeutic doses during pregnancy.

name: Amoxicillin
use_case: Bacterial infections including ear infections, tonsillitis, chest infections, urinary tract infections, and dental abscesses.
mechanism: Beta-lactam antibiotic that inhibits bacterial cell wall synthesis by binding penicillin-binding proteins.

common_dosages.adult: 250–500mg every 8 hours or 500–875mg every 12 hours for 7–14 days.
common_dosages.elderly: Standard adult dose; reduce in renal impairment.

age_warnings.pediatric: Safe in children. Adjust dose by weight. Oral suspension available.
age_warnings.geriatric: Reduce dose if CrCl <30 mL/min.
age_warnings.pregnancy: Category B – generally safe during pregnancy.

name: Ibuprofen
use_case: Pain relief, fever, and inflammation. Used for headaches, dental pain, menstrual cramps, arthritis, muscle pain, and minor injury.
mechanism: Non-selective COX-1 and COX-2 inhibitor. Reduces prostaglandin synthesis, decreasing pain and inflammation.

common_dosages.adult: 200–400mg every 4–6 hours. Maximum 2400mg/day (OTC 1200mg/day).
common_dosages.pediatric: 5–10mg/kg every 6–8 hours. Max 40mg/kg/day. Not for <3 months.
common_dosages.elderly: Use lowest effective dose. Avoid long-term use.

age_warnings.pediatric: Not for infants <3 months. Use with caution in dehydrated children.
age_warnings.geriatric: High risk of GI bleeding and renal impairment. Use minimum dose with PPI cover.
age_warnings.pregnancy: Avoid after 20 weeks – risk of oligohydramnios. Avoid in third trimester.

name: Metformin
use_case: Type 2 diabetes mellitus management. First-line oral antidiabetic agent. Also used off-label for PCOS and prediabetes.
mechanism: Decreases hepatic glucose production, reduces intestinal glucose absorption, and improves insulin sensitivity.

common_dosages.adult: Start 500mg twice daily or 850mg once daily with meals. Max 2550mg/day.
common_dosages.pediatric: 10–16 years: 500mg twice daily with meals. Max 2000mg/day.
common_dosages.elderly: Start low; avoid if eGFR <30. Reduce dose if eGFR 30–45.

age_warnings.pediatric: Approved ≥10 years for T2DM. Not for T1DM.
age_warnings.geriatric: Monitor eGFR annually. Risk of lactic acidosis increases with age.
age_warnings.pregnancy: Insulin preferred in pregnancy; metformin used in some guidelines.

name: Atorvastatin
use_case: Hypercholesterolemia, dyslipidaemia. Reduces LDL cholesterol and triglycerides. Cardiovascular risk reduction.
mechanism: Competitively inhibits HMG-CoA reductase, the rate-limiting enzyme in hepatic cholesterol synthesis.

common_dosages.adult: 10–80mg once daily. Start at 10–20mg; titrate based on LDL targets.
common_dosages.elderly: Standard adult dosing; no specific adjustment.

age_warnings.pediatric: Use only in children ≥10 years with familial hypercholesterolemia.
age_warnings.geriatric: Standard dosing; monitor for muscle symptoms.
age_warnings.pregnancy: Category X – absolutely contraindicated. Teratogenic.

name: Omeprazole
use_case: Gastro-oesophageal reflux disease (GORD/GERD), peptic ulcers, H. pylori eradication, Zollinger-Ellison syndrome, NSAID-induced ulcer prevention.
mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the H+/K+ ATPase proton pump in gastric parietal cells, blocking acid secretion.

common_dosages.adult: 20mg once daily (maintenance); 40mg once daily (acute treatment). Before breakfast.
common_dosages.elderly: Standard adult dose.

age_warnings.pediatric: Use for children ≥1 year; weight-based dosing.
age_warnings.geriatric: Long-term use: monitor for hypomagnesaemia and bone density.
age_warnings.pregnancy: Category C – use only if clearly needed.

name: Lisinopril
use_case: Hypertension, heart failure, diabetic nephropathy, post-MI cardioprotection.
mechanism: Inhibits ACE, preventing conversion of angiotensin I to angiotensin II, resulting in vasodilation and reduced aldosterone secretion.

common_dosages.adult: Hypertension: 10mg once daily (initial); range 10–40mg/day.
common_dosages.elderly: Start at 2.5–5mg/day; titrate carefully.

age_warnings.pediatric: Safety established in children ≥6 years with hypertension.
age_warnings.geriatric: Start at low dose; risk of hypotension and hyperkalaemia.
age_warnings.pregnancy: Category D (2nd/3rd trimester) – causes fetal renal toxicity. Contraindicated.

name: Amlodipine
use_case: Hypertension and angina pectoris. Also used in coronary artery disease.
mechanism: Inhibits calcium influx through L-type calcium channels in vascular smooth muscle and cardiac muscle, causing vasodilation.

common_dosages.adult: 5mg once daily initially; may increase to 10mg once daily.
common_dosages.elderly: Start at 2.5mg; titrate cautiously.

age_warnings.pediatric: Safe ≥6 years for hypertension.
age_warnings.geriatric: Start low; fall risk due to hypotension.
age_warnings.pregnancy: Category C – use only if benefit outweighs risk.

name: Aspirin
use_case: Pain relief, fever, anti-inflammatory. Low-dose (75–100mg) for antiplatelet therapy in cardiovascular disease prevention.
mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits COX-1 and COX-2. Antiplatelet effect via irreversible COX-1 inhibition in platelets.

common_dosages.adult: Pain/fever: 300–900mg every 4–6h (max 4g/day). Antiplatelet: 75–100mg once daily.
common_dosages.elderly: Antiplatelet dose: 75mg once daily with PPI cover.

age_warnings.pediatric: CONTRAINDICATED under 16 years. Risk of Reye's syndrome – potentially fatal encephalopathy and liver damage.
age_warnings.geriatric: High GI bleeding risk. Co-prescribe PPI. Fall/bleeding risk.
age_warnings.pregnancy: Avoid in third trimester. Low-dose under specialist guidance for pre-eclampsia prevention.

name: Cetirizine
use_case: Allergic rhinitis (hay fever), urticaria (hives), chronic idiopathic urticaria, allergic conjunctivitis.
mechanism: Selective peripheral H1 receptor antagonist with minimal CNS penetration. Reduces histamine-mediated symptoms.

common_dosages.adult: 10mg once daily or 5mg twice daily.
common_dosages.elderly: 5mg once daily (if CrCl 11–31 mL/min).

age_warnings.pediatric: Age-appropriate dosing required. Not for infants <2 years (consult doctor).
age_warnings.geriatric: May cause urinary retention in elderly men with BPH. Sedation risk.
age_warnings.pregnancy: Category B – generally considered safe.

name: Metronidazole
use_case: Anaerobic bacterial infections, protozoal infections (Giardia, Trichomonas, Entamoeba), H. pylori (combination), C. difficile, BV.
mechanism: Enters cells, where its nitro group is reduced to reactive intermediates that damage DNA in anaerobic organisms.

common_dosages.adult: 400–500mg three times daily for 5–7 days (infections). 2g single dose (Trichomonas).
common_dosages.elderly: Standard adult dose; use with caution in hepatic impairment.

contraindications: Alcohol consumption during and 48h after treatment

age_warnings.pediatric: Use with caution; adjust by weight.
age_warnings.geriatric: Risk of peripheral neuropathy with prolonged use.
age_warnings.pregnancy: Avoid high-dose systemic use in first trimester. Low-risk in 2nd/3rd trimester at standard doses.

name: Salbutamol
use_case: Relief of acute bronchospasm in asthma, COPD, and exercise-induced bronchospasm. Rescue inhaler.
mechanism: Selectively stimulates beta-2 adrenergic receptors in bronchial smooth muscle, causing bronchodilation.

common_dosages.adult: Inhaler: 1–2 puffs (100mcg each) every 4–6h as needed. Nebuliser: 2.5mg in 2.5mL.
common_dosages.pediatric: Inhaler: 1–2 puffs every 4–6h. Nebuliser: 1.25–2.5mg under medical supervision.
common_dosages.elderly: Standard dose; monitor heart rate.

age_warnings.pediatric: Safe for all ages; use spacer device for children <5 years.
age_warnings.geriatric: Monitor cardiac side effects; pre-existing arrhythmias.
age_warnings.pregnancy: Category C – commonly used; benefits outweigh risks in uncontrolled asthma.

schedule: Prescription only (inhaler); OTC in some countries

name: Codeine
use_case: Mild to moderate pain (usually in combination products). Cough suppression. Diarrhoea.
mechanism: Prodrug metabolised to morphine by CYP2D6. Binds mu-opioid receptors, reducing pain transmission and cough reflex.

common_dosages.adult: 15–60mg every 4 hours for pain. Max 240mg/day.
common_dosages.pediatric: CONTRAINDICATED in children <12 years. Avoid in children 12–18 with respiratory issues.
common_dosages.elderly: Start at lowest dose; risk of respiratory depression and constipation.

age_warnings.pediatric: CONTRAINDICATED under 12 years. FDA black box warning: deaths reported in children after tonsillectomy. Ultra-rapid CYP2D6 metabolisers produce fatal morphine levels.
age_warnings.geriatric: Start at 25–50% of normal dose. Monitor for respiratory depression and constipation.
age_warnings.pregnancy: Category C; chronic use causes neonatal abstinence syndrome. Avoid near delivery.

name: Diazepam
use_case: Anxiety disorders, alcohol withdrawal, seizures (status epilepticus), muscle spasm, sedation before procedures.
mechanism: Potentiates GABA-A receptor activity, enhancing chloride ion influx, causing CNS depression.

common_dosages.adult: Anxiety: 2–10mg 2–4 times daily. Seizures: 5–10mg IV. Muscle spasm: 5mg TID.
common_dosages.pediatric: Seizures: 0.1–0.3mg/kg IV slowly. Only under specialist guidance.
common_dosages.elderly: 2.5–5mg; maximum 10mg/day. Clearance reduced by 50%.

age_warnings.pediatric: Only for seizure emergencies in children <6 months. Risk of respiratory arrest.
age_warnings.geriatric: HIGH-RISK in elderly (Beers Criteria). Risk of falls, hip fractures, cognitive decline. Reduce dose by 50%.
age_warnings.pregnancy: Category D – neonatal withdrawal and floppy infant syndrome. Avoid in 1st trimester.

name: Levothyroxine
use_case: Hypothyroidism, thyroid cancer (suppression therapy), myxoedema coma.
mechanism: Synthetic T4 that is converted to active T3 peripherally, restoring normal thyroid hormone levels.

common_dosages.adult: Start 25–50mcg/day; increase by 25–50mcg every 4–6 weeks. Target TSH. Average maintenance 100–200mcg/day.
common_dosages.elderly: Start 12.5–25mcg; titrate slowly. Cardiac risk.

age_warnings.pediatric: Critical for neonatal development. Congenital hypothyroidism treatment must begin immediately.
age_warnings.geriatric: Start very low; cardiac arrhythmias common if dose escalated too fast.
age_warnings.pregnancy: Requirements increase 25–50% during pregnancy. Monitor TSH every trimester.

name: Ciprofloxacin
use_case: Urinary tract infections, respiratory tract infections, skin/soft tissue infections, bone/joint infections, intraabdominal infections, anthrax post-exposure.
mechanism: Inhibits bacterial DNA gyrase and topoisomerase IV, preventing DNA replication and transcription.

common_dosages.adult: 250–750mg twice daily (oral). 200–400mg every 8–12h (IV). Duration 3–14 days.
common_dosages.pediatric: Complicated UTI/pyelonephritis only: 10–20mg/kg twice daily. NOT for routine infections.
common_dosages.elderly: Reduce dose if CrCl <30. Risk of tendon rupture.

age_warnings.pediatric: Avoid in children except for specific indications (anthrax, complicated UTI) – arthropathy risk.
age_warnings.geriatric: High risk of Achilles tendon rupture, especially with corticosteroids.
age_warnings.pregnancy: Category C – avoid; teratogenic animal data.

name: Prednisolone
use_case: Inflammatory conditions, autoimmune diseases, asthma exacerbations, allergic reactions, nephrotic syndrome, IBD, rheumatoid arthritis.
mechanism: Binds glucocorticoid receptors; reduces synthesis of inflammatory mediators (cytokines, prostaglandins). Immunosuppressive.

common_dosages.adult: 5–60mg/day depending on condition. Taper when stopping long-term use.
common_dosages.elderly: Use minimum effective dose; high risk of side effects.

age_warnings.pediatric: Growth suppression with prolonged use. Use minimum dose for minimum duration.
age_warnings.geriatric: High risk of osteoporosis, fractures, diabetes, and hypertension.
age_warnings.pregnancy: Category C – used for fetal lung maturity (betamethasone preferred). Gestational diabetes risk.

name: Sertraline
use_case: Major depressive disorder, panic disorder, OCD, PTSD, social anxiety disorder, premenstrual dysphoric disorder (PMDD).
mechanism: Selectively inhibits serotonin transporter (SERT), increasing synaptic serotonin levels in CNS.

common_dosages.adult: Start 50mg once daily; may increase to 200mg/day. Full effect in 4–6 weeks.
common_dosages.pediatric: OCD in children 6–17 years: start 25mg/day (6–12y) or 50mg/day (13–17y).
common_dosages.elderly: Start 25–50mg; standard dosing generally tolerated.

age_warnings.pediatric: FDA BLACK BOX: increased risk of suicidal thinking in children/adolescents. Monitor closely in first weeks. NOT approved for MDD in <18.
age_warnings.geriatric: Hyponatraemia risk. Start at 25mg. Monitor sodium levels.
age_warnings.pregnancy: Category C – neonatal adaptation syndrome with third-trimester use. Persistent pulmonary hypertension risk.

name: Warfarin
use_case: Prevention and treatment of venous thromboembolism, atrial fibrillation stroke prevention, mechanical heart valve prophylaxis.
mechanism: Inhibits vitamin K epoxide reductase, preventing activation of vitamin K-dependent clotting factors (II, VII, IX, X) and proteins C and S.

common_dosages.adult: Highly individualised. Typical 2–10mg/day. Dose adjusted by INR (target 2.0–3.0 for most indications; 2.5–3.5 for mechanical valves).
common_dosages.pediatric: Specialist use only. Weight-based with frequent INR monitoring.
common_dosages.elderly: Require lower doses; more sensitive to warfarin effects.

contraindications: Unsupervised patients at high fall risk
interactions: Herbal products (garlic, ginkgo, St John's Wort)

age_warnings.pediatric: Specialist use only. Requires very frequent INR monitoring.
age_warnings.geriatric: Very high bleeding risk. Frequent INR monitoring. Falls = major risk.
age_warnings.pregnancy: Category X – ABSOLUTELY CONTRAINDICATED. Causes warfarin embryopathy, fetal haemorrhage.

name: Insulin
use_case: Type 1 diabetes (essential), Type 2 diabetes (when oral agents insufficient), diabetic ketoacidosis, hyperglycaemic hyperosmolar state.
mechanism: Binds insulin receptors, facilitating cellular glucose uptake, inhibiting gluconeogenesis, and promoting glycogen/lipid synthesis.

common_dosages.adult: Highly individualised based on blood glucose. Typical starting: 0.2–0.5 units/kg/day SC.
common_dosages.pediatric: 0.5–1.0 unit/kg/day initially; adjusted per glucose monitoring.
common_dosages.elderly: Use lower doses; hypoglycaemia risk is high and dangerous.

age_warnings.pediatric: Type 1 DM: insulin is life-sustaining. Must use insulin analogue pens with paediatric doses.
age_warnings.geriatric: High hypoglycaemia risk. Simplify regimen. Use glucose-monitoring devices.
age_warnings.pregnancy: Insulin is the PREFERRED agent for diabetes in pregnancy. Safe for fetus.

schedule: Prescription only (analogue insulins); OTC in some countries (human insulins)
